sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.28 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite simple transitive permutation group, and let$(G)$be the proportion of derangements in$G$. Is it true that$(G) 89/325$? Note that$(G) = 89/325$for the action of the Tits group$G = {}^2F_4(2)'$on the cosets of a maximal parabolic subgroup of the form$2^2.[2^8].S_3$(Forum Math. Sigma, 13 (2025), paper no.\,e98, 62 pp.). { T.\,C.\,Burness, M.\,Fusari}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
